title: Aromadendron gustavi
taxonomy: Aromadendron gustavi is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to New Caledonia, a French territory in the Pacific Ocean. Aromadendron gustavi belongs to the genus Aromadendron, which is a group of plants commonly known as the "scented apricot trees" due to their aromatic flowers. The plant is a small to medium-sized tree that produces showy white flowers and yellowish-red fruit. Aromadendron gustavi is considered an endemic species, meaning it is found only in its native region of New Caledonia.
uses: Aromadendron gustavi is a plant species, but information on its medicinal uses is limited.
